title: Add separate login pages and dashboards for Hotel, NGO, Volunteer, and Admin entities

requirements.text: Extend the backend data model to support entity-type-specific user registration and authentication. Add an `entityType` field (variant: #hotel, #ngo, #volunteer, #admin) to the UserProfile type. Provide backend endpoints to register and retrieve profiles by entity type, and a dedicated admin role check for protected operations.

quotes: add login for different entitites like hotel,ngo,volenteer,admin

acceptanceCriteria: UserProfile includes an `entityType` field supporting hotel, ngo, volunteer, and admin variants
Backend exposes a registerProfile or updateProfile endpoint that accepts entityType
Admin-only endpoints reject calls from non-admin principals

requirements.text: Create a unified login landing page at `/login` that displays four distinct login entry points: Hotel, NGO, Volunteer, and Admin. Each entry point should visually represent the entity with an icon and label, and navigate to that entity's dedicated login page on click.

quotes: add login for different entitites like hotel,ngo,volenteer,admin and create seprate login,pages

acceptanceCriteria: A `/login` route renders a page with four clearly labeled entity cards: Hotel, NGO, Volunteer, Admin
Clicking each card navigates to the corresponding dedicated login page
The page is accessible without authentication

requirements.text: Create a dedicated Hotel login page at `/login/hotel`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `hotel` upon first login, and redirect to a Hotel-specific dashboard after successful login.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/login/hotel` exists and renders a Hotel-branded login page
Clicking login triggers Internet Identity authentication
On successful login, entityType is saved as `hotel` in the user profile
User is redirected to `/hotel-dashboard` after login

requirements.text: Create a dedicated NGO login page at `/login/ngo`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `ngo` upon first login, and redirect to the existing NGO dashboard after successful login.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/login/ngo` exists and renders an NGO-branded login page
Clicking login triggers Internet Identity authentication
On successful login, entityType is saved as `ngo` in the user profile
User is redirected to `/ngo-dashboard` after login

requirements.text: Create a dedicated Volunteer login page at `/login/volunteer`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `volunteer` upon first login, and redirect to the existing Volunteer dashboard after successful login.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/login/volunteer` exists and renders a Volunteer-branded login page
Clicking login triggers Internet Identity authentication
On successful login, entityType is saved as `volunteer` in the user profile
User is redirected to `/volunteer-dashboard` after login

requirements.text: Create a dedicated Admin login page at `/login/admin`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `admin` upon first login, and redirect to the existing Admin Analytics dashboard after successful login.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/login/admin` exists and renders an Admin-branded login page
Clicking login triggers Internet Identity authentication
On successful login, entityType is saved as `admin` in the user profile
User is redirected to `/admin` after login

requirements.text: Create a Hotel-specific dashboard page at `/hotel-dashboard`. It should display hotel-relevant actions such as posting surplus food donations and viewing donation history. Protect this route so only users with entityType `hotel` can access it; others should be redirected to `/login/hotel`.

quotes: add login for different entitites like hotel

acceptanceCriteria: Route `/hotel-dashboard` exists and is accessible only to users with entityType `hotel`
Dashboard displays hotel-specific sections: post food donation, view past donations
Non-hotel users are redirected to `/login/hotel`

requirements.text: Update the `RoleGuard` component and the `useRoleBasedAccess` hook to handle the new `hotel` entity type in addition to the existing roles, granting hotel users access to `/hotel-dashboard` and the food donation form.

quotes: add login for different entitites like hotel,ngo,volenteer,admin

acceptanceCriteria: `useRoleBasedAccess` returns `hotel` as a valid role derived from entityType
`RoleGuard` correctly permits hotel users to access hotel-specific routes
Existing NGO, volunteer, and admin role guards remain unaffected

requirements.text: Update the main navigation bar in `App.tsx` to show entity-appropriate navigation links based on the logged-in user's entityType. Hotels see donation-related links; NGOs see NGO dashboard; Volunteers see volunteer dashboard; Admins see admin analytics. Unauthenticated users see a link to `/login`.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation links change based on the authenticated user's entityType
Unauthenticated users see a 'Login' link pointing to `/login`
Each entity type sees only the navigation links relevant to their role

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All authentication must use Internet Identity — no username/password flows
Existing NGO, Volunteer, and Admin dashboards must remain functional after changes
nonGoals: Building a separate backend service per entity type
Real-time notifications or messaging between entities
Changing the existing food safety engine or NGO matching logic
